sourceMovie.title: Role Play
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Role Play (2023) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Thomas Vincent's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mr. & Mrs. Smith
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Role Play clicked for you, Mr. & Mrs. Smith (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Role Play?
faq.answer: Try Mr. & Mrs. Smith, 10 Things I Hate About You, Shall We Dance?—each shares DNA with Role Play while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Role Play?
faq.answer: Role Play (2023) is best described as Action, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Role Play worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
